stem: An insertion sort is being used to sort the list [4, 1, 5, 2] into ascending order.
model_answer: Treat the first element (4) as a sorted sub-list. Take the next element (1), compare with 4: 1 < 4, so shift 4 right and insert 1 at the front → [1, 4, 5, 2]. Take 5, compare with 4: 5 > 4, leave in place → [1, 4, 5, 2]. Take 2, compare with 5 (shift), then with 4 (shift), then with 1 (stop, 2 > 1) → insert 2 between 1 and 4 → [1, 2, 4, 5]. The list is now sorted.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: take next element, compare backwards through sorted prefix, shift larger items right, insert in correct position, applied to the example values.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how insertion sort would sort the list [4, 1, 5, 2] into ascending order. Use numbered steps and show the list after each pass.

mark_points.text: treats the first element as a sorted sub-list and walks through the rest one element at a time
compares the new element backwards through the sorted prefix
shifts items greater than the new element one position to the right
inserts the element in its correct position; uses the example values to show at least one intermediate state
